tavily.answer: Recent developments in South Sudan indicate escalating violence and ongoing humanitarian crises. A government-appointed county commissioner was assassinated by opposition forces, threatening the implementation of the 2018 peace agreement. In Sudan, nearly 20 million people face acute hunger due to civil war and deteriorating infrastructure. The United States has imposed visa bans on South Sudanese officials for undermining the peace deal and engaging in corruption. Over 300 children have been killed or injured in the ongoing war in Sudan, primarily due to drone strikes, highlighting the severe impact on civilians.
